column1: TheSparky: Last I checked, I'm 29th Kyu
C[TheSparky: I'm sure it shows, but I have no idea about strategy in this game
Phelan: there's not much strategy in these small boards, mostly tactics
Phelan: either way, still too early to tell
Phelan: so far you haven't made any mistake
C[Phelan: did you forget about this game?
rgatti: Thanks for challenging me!  I'm enjoying learning this game via playing!
rgatti: Oooh!  They added timestamps!  That's awesome!
TheSparky: I'm here for the lulz
C[rgatti: Yeah, so far, I've only played with Phel (who is much better than me) and ohgrande (who seems about as new as I am).
rgatti: I'm enjoying playing, but I learn a lot with each game (seeing things, including errors, play out... versus reading about ideal play/position in some book or website)
TheSparky: I've only played with Phel as well.
TheSparky: He is kicking my ass
TheSparky: Go is one of those games where a huge difference in skill level is immensely difficult to overcome, regardless of handicaps
TheSparky: I mean, I could start with 9 extra stones on the board, but if I don't know the strategies, then I may as well not have them
C[rgatti: It is certainly a game I'm learning a lot about when I'm playing
rgatti: Mostly learning that whatever I just did against Phel was wrong... and a horrible decision
rgatti: but still learning... kind of
rgatti: And then repeating those mistakes to remind myself why that wasn't the right thing to do
C[TheSparky: I'm sure this is wrong, but it amuses me
C[TheSparky: So, every move I plan out ends horribly for me
